step_by_step_thinking:
I agree that obesity significantly contributes to hypertension largely through the actions of aldosterone increasing sodium reabsorption, thus promoting fluid retention. Another important factor contributing to hypertension in obese individuals involves the secretion of pro-inflammatory cytokines from adipose tissue, which can impair endothelial function and increase arterial stiffness. These inflammatory mediators further exacerbate cardiovascular risks beyond just salt sensitivity mediated by aldosterone alone.

Answer: D

raw_response:
I agree that obesity significantly contributes to hypertension largely through the actions of aldosterone increasing sodium reabsorption, thus promoting fluid retention. Another important factor contributing to hypertension in obese individuals involves the secretion of pro-inflammatory cytokines from adipose tissue, which can impair endothelial function and increase arterial stiffness. These inflammatory mediators further exacerbate cardiovascular risks beyond just salt sensitivity mediated by aldosterone alone.

Answer: D

Your analysis adds depth by incorporating the role of inflammation driven by adipose tissues, offering a broader perspective on pathophysiological mechanisms involved in hypertensive development seen specifically in overweight populations.
